Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,000 (In addition, about 275 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Windsor Queen Xtrabed Mobile Murphy Bed (closed)
Recalled Windsor Queen Xtrabed Mobile Murphy Bed (open)
Left: Models after May 2022 (with notch) - Right: Models prior to May 2022 (without notch)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the Xtrabed's outer case breaking and falling when opened from the front, resulting in 11 injuries to consumers' shoulders, back, legs, knees or feet. The firm has received one report of a tip-over resulting in bruising to the arm.

Retailers.Name: Online at www.wayfair.com from November 2016 through April 2023 for between $1,500 and $1,860.

Importers.Name: SourceOne Ventures Inc., of Anaheim, California

Hazards.Name: The outer case or cabinet on the front, or smaller side, of the Xtrabed can break and fall when opening the bed from the front, posing an injury hazard. Further, the Xtrabed can tip over if not anchored to the wall, posing tip-over and entrapment hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Xtrabeds and contact J Grubb Designs, a wholly owned subsidiary of SourceOne Ventures, to schedule a free home repair by a technician. Consumers who opt to not receive the repair can instead select a full refund. J Grubb Designs is contacting all known purchasers directly.

recalls.Description: This recall involves all sizes of the Nap Queen 12-inch Maxima Hybrid of Cooling Gel Memory Foam and Bonnell Spring Mattress, including model numbers NQIH12KK, NQIH12QQ, NQIH12FF, NQIH12TT and NQIH12TX. The mattress is single-sided and has white with gray-dotted fabric ticking, a dark gray 4-inch mesh stripe and wave quilted pattern on the sides, and a straight-line quilted pattern on the top of the mattress. The tape edges are white and gray striped. On the mattress label, sewn into the cover, is the model name "Maxima" with the model number and "Imported by Adven Group, LLC." The mattresses were sold compressed in a box.
recalls.Title: Nap Queen Maxima Hybrid Mattresses Recalled by Adven Group Due to Fire Hazard; Violation of Federal Flammability Standard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Nap Queen toll-free at 866-387-6239 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, or online at https://sleepqueens.myshopify.com/pages/napqueen-maxima-12-twin-size-mattress-recall or www.napqueensleep.com and click on the "Product Recall" banner at the top of the page for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nap Queen 12-inch Maxima Hybrid Mattress
Close-up of fabric patterns on side and top of the mattress

Retailers.Name: Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Overstock.com from February 2023 through May 2023 for between $170 and $350.

Importers.Name: Adven Group LLC, of Missouri City, Texas

Hazards.Name: The mattresses fail to meet the mandatory federal flammability standard for mattresses, posing a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled mattresses and contact Nap Queen to receive a free fitted cover to put on the mattress to bring it into compliance with the federal standard.
